title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · API and Operations Recall
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs services, endpointslices, dns, discovery, and traffic policies from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the service discovery and endpoint evidence map under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for service discovery and endpoint evidence map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.
Trace Service selectors, EndpointSlices, readiness and serving conditions, manual endpoints, topology hints, and stale endpoint diagnosis.
Choose ClusterIP, headless, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName, or selectorless Service from discovery, exposure, platform, and security constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review K8s Pod Network Identity
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s Pod Network Identity orchestration evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review K8s Pod Network Identity.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s Pod Network Identity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review K8s Pod Network Identity, keep K8s Pod Network Identity connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A review Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so K8s Pod Network Identity has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Explain Pod IP identity, node routing, ports, host network exceptions, replacement, dual-stack boundaries, and application bind behavior.
